title: The Army Is Burning Through Its AI Tokens
gist: The US Army exhausted its 100-million-token annual Ask Sage AI subscription within weeks of offering 'unlimited' access, forcing re-imposed limits—part of a broader pattern of enterprise AI adopters (Meta, Uber) blowing through token budgets far faster than planned while questioning the tools' actual usefulness.

insights.claim: The Army CIO announced unlimited AI tokens in May 2026 but by mid-June the CIO pool was exhausted and limits had to be re-established, with an employee saying the whole Army burned through a full year of tokens for just one service.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: Demonstrates that 'unlimited' enterprise AI offers are financially unsustainable once usage is unconstrained.
insights.quote: Apparently the whole Army burned through the whole year of tokens for just one service

insights.claim: The Army bought 100,000,000 tokens as an annual Ask Sage enterprise subscription, gave employees at least 200,000 tokens per month with automatic top-ups, and even emailed under-users to encourage more consumption before the pool ran dry.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: Actively pushing consumption then hitting caps reveals poor demand forecasting in government AI procurement.
insights.locator: In order to use Ask Sage

insights.claim: It is unclear whether the Army CIO token pool will be renewed after October 1, and whether regular DOD employee tokens draw from the same pool as classified or secret AI usage.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: Budget uncertainty and pooled classified/unclassified usage flag governance gaps in federal AI deployment.
insights.locator: “Although the Army CIO announced in May 2026

insights.claim: The DOD said nearly half of its 3.5 million employees were using AI at work, and burned through some 20 billion tokens per day during the 38-day Operation Epic Fury in Iran, per Breaking Defense.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: Wartime token consumption at 20B/day illustrates the massive compute demand and cost curve of operational military AI.
insights.locator: The Defense Department burned through some 20 billion tokens

insights.claim: Ask Sage is a multimodal platform accredited for Controlled Unclassified Information that lets Army users run Alphabet's Gemini, Meta's Llama, and OpenAI's ChatGPT, and is used by the DOD's Chief Digital and AI Office for acquisitions.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: A model-agnostic government AI aggregator is a channel worth watching for the major LLM vendors' federal revenue.
insights.locator: The Army uses Ask Sage, a multimodal generative AI platform

insights.claim: The Pentagon cut staff at its Civilian Protection Center of Excellence, whose jobs entailed preventing civilian casualties, and is instead developing an AI tool to speed up those assessments.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: Substituting AI for human casualty-assessment staff signals both cost-cutting appetite and rising ethical risk exposure.

insights.claim: After encouraging employees to 'tokenmaxx', Meta quietly took down its token-usage leaderboard and is now trying to curb use, with Instagram head Adam Mosseri floating a per-engineer token cap.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: Even AI-native firms are reversing unlimited-usage policies, signaling a broad shift toward AI cost discipline.
insights.locator: The Army is not the first eager adopter

insights.claim: Uber engineers burned through a year's worth of generative AI tokens in merely four months, per Fortune reporting.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: 3x-faster-than-budgeted burn across multiple large enterprises points to a systemic underpricing of AI usage forecasts.
insights.locator: According to reporting from Fortune, Uber also saw

insights.claim: An Army employee found the generative AI tools not particularly useful and unreliable for their work, with one model asserting it had completed a task it hadn't.
insights.speaker: Vittoria Elliott
insights.so_what: Frontline reliability complaints undercut the ROI case for aggressive token consumption in bureaucracies.
insights.quote: an unthinking application and use is not going to result in an effective, efficient, and trustworthy rollout
insights.locator: The Army employee says they have not found
